corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

location.sentence: Our experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of ANCE on web search, question answering, and in a commercial search envi- ronment, showing ANCE dot-product retrieval nearly matches the accuracy of BERT-based cascade IR pipeline, while being 100x more efficient.

context_window.previous: We then propose Approximate nearest neighbor Negative Contrastive Learning (ANCE), a learning mechanism that selects hard training negatives globally from the entire corpus, using an asyn- chronously updated ANN index.
context_window.current: Our experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of ANCE on web search, question answering, and in a commercial search envi- ronment, showing ANCE dot-product retrieval nearly matches the accuracy of BERT-based cascade IR pipeline, while being 100x more efficient.

location.sentence: More Details on Baselines: The most representative sparse retrieval baselines in TREC DL include the standard BM25 (“bm25base” or “bm25base_p”), Best TREC Sparse Retrieval (“bm25tuned_rm3” or “bm25tuned_prf_p”) with tuned query expansion (Lavrenko & Croft, 2017), and Best DeepCT (“dct_tp_bm25e2”, doc only), which uses BERT to estimate the term importance for BM25 (Dai & Callan, 2019a).

top_candidates.sentence: The document corpus was post-constructed by back-filling the body texts of the passage's URLs and their labels were inherited from its passages .
TREC DL official metrics include NDCG@10 on test and MRR@10 on MARCO Passage Dev.
MARCO Document Dev is noisy and the recall on the DL Track testing is less meaningful due to low label coverage on DR results.

We then propose Approximate nearest neighbor Negative Contrastive Learning (ANCE), a learning mechanism that selects hard training negatives globally from the entire corpus, using an asynchronously updated ANN index.

We then propose Approximate nearest neighbor Negative Contrastive Learning (ANCE), a learning mechanism that selects hard training negatives globally from the entire corpus, using an asynchronously updated ANN index.

Our experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of ANCE on web search, question answering, and in a commercial search environment, showing ANCE dot-product retrieval nearly matches the accuracy of BERT-based cascade IR pipeline, while being 100x more efficient.
A key challenge in DR is to construct proper negative instances during its representation learning .

Unlike in reranking where negatives are naturally the irrelevant documents from previous retrieval stages, in first stage retrieval, DR models have to distinguish relevant documents from all irrelevant ones in the entire corpus.
In addition, the accuracy of dense retrieval models often underperform BM25, especially on documents .
This work demonstrates that a simple dense retrieval system can achieve SOTA accuracy, while also behaves dramatically different from classic retrieval.
Instead of a bigger local pool, ANCE goes all the way along this trajectory and constructs negatives globally from the entire corpus, using an asynchronously updated ANN index.
We then propose ANCE to eliminate this bottleneck by constructing training negatives globally from the entire corpus.
We then propose ANCE to eliminate this bottleneck by constructing training negatives globally from the entire corpus.
This enlarged negative pool significantly improves unsupervised visual representation learning .
Its better retrieval accuracy can benefit many language systems.
But the retrieval accuracy is not much better.

downstream_contract.intended_use: Feed this JSON to a later local open-source model to write an overview.
downstream_contract.grounding_rule: The downstream generator should write from evidence_units.location.sentence and context_window, not from unsupported outside knowledge.
downstream_contract.score_warning: Scores are ranking and filtering signals, not calibrated probabilities.
